seoTitle: Import Credit Card Transactions into QuickBooks
metaDescription: How to manually upload credit card statement files into QuickBooks Online when automatic bank feeds fail or don't cover the full date range.
intro: When a live bank feed drops or only pulls the last 90 days of history, you can still get your credit card data into QuickBooks Online by uploading a statement file directly. This guide covers downloading the right file format, mapping its columns, and fixing common formatting issues like reversed charge signs.

steps.heading: Download your statement file
steps.body: Log into your card issuer's portal and export your transactions as a QBO, QFX, or CSV file covering the date range you need.

steps.heading: Start the upload in QuickBooks
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, and use the Link Account dropdown to select Upload from File instead of connecting live.

steps.heading: Choose the destination account
steps.body: Pick the existing credit card liability account in your Chart of Accounts that the imported data should post to.

steps.heading: Map your columns
steps.body: Match your file's date, description, and amount columns to QuickBooks' fields so the data lines up correctly.

steps.heading: Check charge signs and import
steps.body: Confirm whether charges show as negative and payments as positive; if your file has it backwards, check Reverse all values before completing the import.

faq.question: Why would I manually import instead of using a live bank feed?
faq.answer: Manual import is useful when a live connection breaks, or when you need older transaction history than the feed's typical 90-day window covers.

faq.question: What file formats does QuickBooks accept?
faq.answer: Standard QBO, QFX, and CSV files exported from most banking portals work for this kind of upload.

faq.question: My charges are showing as positive numbers — is that a problem?
faq.answer: If your card's export format flips the usual sign convention, use the Reverse all values checkbox during import to correct it.

faq.question: Do imported transactions still need categorizing?
faq.answer: Yes, once imported they land in the For Review queue just like feed transactions, ready to be matched or categorized.
